title: Writing proportions
description: Practice writing proportions to describe real-world situations.
topic_path: Topic B: Unit rate and constant of proportionality

assessment_items.question_content:
$4$ glue sticks cost $\$7.76$. 

 ** Which equation would help determine the cost of $13$ glue sticks? **

[[â˜ƒ radio 1]]
hints: We can write the fact that $4$ glue sticks cost $\$7.76$ as a proportion:
$\qquad \dfrac{4}{\$7.76}$
Let $x$ represent the unknown cost of $13$ glue sticks. Since $13$ glue sticks cost $x$, we have the following proportion:
$\qquad \dfrac{13}{x}$
The cost changes along with the number of glue sticks purchased, and so the two proportions are equivalent.
$\qquad \dfrac{4}{\$7.76} = \dfrac{13}{x}$

$9$ pencils cost $\$7.74$. 

** Which equation would help determine the cost of $6$ pencils? **

[[â˜ƒ radio 1]]

assessment_items.question_content:
$4$ markers cost $\$7.04$. 

** Which equation would help determine the cost of $7$ markers? **

[[â˜ƒ radio 1]]
hints: We know the cost of $4$ markers. We want to know the cost of $7$ markers. We can write the numbers of markers as a proportion:
$\qquad \dfrac{4}{7}$
We know $4$ markers costs $\$7.04$. We can let $x$ represent the unknown cost of $7$ markers. The proportion of these costs can be expressed as:
$\qquad \dfrac{\$7.04}{x}$
The cost changes along with the number of markers purchased, and so the two proportions are equivalent.
$\qquad \dfrac{4}{7} = \dfrac{\$7.04}{x}$

$\qquad \dfrac{5}{11} = \dfrac{\$9.70}{x}$

assessment_items.question_content:
$6$ erasers cost $\$6.60$. 

** Which equation would help determine the cost of $3$ erasers? **

[[â˜ƒ radio 1]]
hints: If we let $x$ represent the cost of $3$ erasers, we have the following proportion:
$\qquad \dfrac{x}{3}$
We have to pay $\$6.60$ for $6$ erasers, and that can be written as a proportion:
$\qquad \dfrac{\$6.60}{6}$
Since the price per eraser stays the same, these two proportions are equivalent.
$\qquad \dfrac{x}{3} = \dfrac{\$6.60}{6}$
